chunk_summary: This section emphasizes linking dietary findings with targeted investigations, prioritizing tailored nutrition plans and specific tests based on clinical discordance or symptoms, avoiding unnecessary broad panels.
learning_objectives: Understand when to trigger targeted investigations based on dietary findings and clinical discordance.
Learn to prioritize nutrition and feeding plans before ordering broad laboratory panels.
Identify first-line tests for specific conditions such as anemia, malabsorption, or systemic disease.
Contextualize investigations to local epidemiology and clinical relevance.
key_facts: Investigations should follow clinical discordance, such as persistent growth faltering despite adequate intake over 2–4 weeks.
First-line tests include CBC and ferritin for anemia, urine analysis for occult UTI, and celiac serology for gluten exposure.
Persistent microcytic anemia requires evaluation for chronic inflammation, blood loss, hemoglobinopathy, or celiac disease.
In obesity, endocrine evaluation is relevant when weight gain is paired with poor linear growth or pubertal delay.
In India, investigations should consider local risks like tuberculosis, celiac disease, and parasitic infections.
reference_quote: Faltering growth: recognition and management (NICE NG75) emphasizes prioritizing nutrition plans and targeted investigations over broad panels in cases of growth faltering.
document_key_topics: Dietary assessment and feeding skills evaluation
First-line tests for specific clinical conditions
